skill.name: google-maps-reviews-api-skill
skill.description: Automatically extract reviews from Google Maps via the Google Maps Reviews API. Use for local business analysis, reputation monitoring, competitive benchmarking, and sentiment analysis.
skill.summary: Automate Google Maps review extraction using the BrowserAct API to get structured review data with ratings, text, dates, and author information.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Security evaluation confirms the skill makes legitimate API calls to BrowserAct (api.browseract.com) using an API key read from environment variables. The Python script is well-structured with proper error handling, retry logic, and no obfuscation. All 62 static findings were evaluated: the majority (48) are false positives from pattern-matching in markdown documentation and print statements. The 14 true positive findings confirm expected risk factors (network, env_access, external_commands) that are necessary for the skill's legitimate function. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. Recommended for publication with a medium risk warning.

medium_findings.title: External API Access with Authentication Credentials
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com with a Bearer token API key in the Authorization header. Network calls are made to start tasks, poll status, and retrieve results. This is the core functionality of the skill and is legitimate, but users should be aware that their API key is transmitted to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network requests with Bearer auth header are clearly visible at three locations. The endpoint is a legitimate workflow automation API. This is expected and necessary behavior for the skill's stated purpose.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
medium_findings.description: The skill reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables using os.getenv(). This is the standard and recommended pattern for handling secrets, but the combination with network access means the API key is exposed to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct call to os.getenv('BROWSERACT_API_KEY') confirmed at line 93. Standard and secure pattern for API key handling, no hardcoded secrets.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Instructed External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The SKILL.md instructs the AI agent to execute a Python script as a shell command with user-provided parameters. While the Python script safely handles command-line arguments via sys.argv, users should be aware that the agent will run external code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear bash code block in SKILL.md instructs agent to run python command with user-provided search keywords. The Python script uses sys.argv which is safe from shell injection, but the agent constructs the command.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation and Print Statements
low_findings.description: URLs to BrowserAct API and console are present in help text and documentation. These are not malicious but are static references that could become outdated.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are in print statements and documentation, not actual network requests. They serve as user guidance for obtaining an API key.

low_findings.title: Error Response Content Disclosure
low_findings.description: The Python script prints raw API response content in error messages, which could potentially expose internal details if the API returns sensitive information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 45 prints the full API response in error messages. While unlikely to expose sensitive data from this particular API, it is a minor information disclosure concern.

content.value_statement: Manually collecting customer reviews from Google Maps is slow and error-prone. This skill automates review extraction using the BrowserAct API, returning structured data with ratings, review text, dates, and author profiles.
actual_capabilities: Extract reviews from Google Maps for any search query or business type
Return structured data including star ratings, review text, dates, and author names
Support multiple languages and country-specific search result filtering
Poll task status and retrieve results automatically with timeout handling
Detect and report invalid or expired API keys without retrying
Handle network failures and task errors with automatic single retry
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key to access the Google Maps Reviews API
Task execution may take several minutes depending on search volume and API availability
Limited to data available through the BrowserAct Google Maps Reviews API template
Automatic retry is limited to one attempt per task to prevent infinite loops

use_cases.title: Monitor Business Reputation
use_cases.description: Track customer reviews for a specific business location over time. Extract recent reviews to monitor rating changes, identify emerging complaints, and respond to customer feedback.

use_cases.title: Analyze Competitor Reviews
use_cases.description: Extract and compare reviews across competitor businesses in the same industry. Identify strengths and weaknesses by analyzing recurring themes in customer feedback.
use_cases.target_user: Market researchers and competitive intelligence analysts

use_cases.title: Qualify Service Providers
use_cases.description: Research and evaluate local service providers by collecting their Google Maps reviews. Compare ratings, read detailed feedback, and identify the highest-quality businesses in a specific area.

prompt_templates.title: Extract reviews for a single business type
prompt_templates.prompt: Find and extract Google Maps reviews for coffee shops in downtown Austin. Search for 'coffee shop' in English with country set to US.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to research local coffee shops before opening a new location.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor reviews for a specific brand
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the latest Google Maps reviews for Tesla showrooms in California. Search for 'Tesla showroom' in English and filter by country US. Show me the most recent reviews first.
prompt_templates.scenario: A brand manager wants to track customer feedback across showrooms.

prompt_templates.title: Compare reviews in multiple languages
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Google Maps reviews for dental clinics. First search in English for US results, then search the same keywords in Spanish for Mexico. Compare the ratings and common complaints between both countries.
prompt_templates.scenario: A healthcare provider wants to compare service quality across regions.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk competitive analysis across multiple businesses
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract Google Maps reviews for three competing pizza restaurants near downtown Chicago. For each restaurant, get the average rating, total review count, most common positive keywords, and most common complaints. Present the data in a comparison table.
prompt_templates.scenario: A restaurant owner wants to benchmark their business against local competitors.

output_examples.input: Search for 'sushi restaurant' in English with country set to US for downtown San Francisco.
output_examples.output: The skill extracted 47 reviews from Google Maps for sushi restaurants in downtown San Francisco. Results included an average rating of 4.2 stars, with the most recent reviews mentioning fresh fish quality, wait times, and lunch specials. Review dates ranged from the past 3 months with author names and star ratings included.

output_examples.input: Compare reviews for two hardware stores in Seattle: one local and one chain.
output_examples.output: The skill retrieved reviews for both hardware stores in Seattle. The local store showed a 4.5-star average with frequent praise for staff knowledge and personalized service. The chain store had a 3.8-star average with common complaints about long checkout lines and limited stock availability. Ten recent reviews were analyzed for each location.

best_practices: Set your BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable before running the skill to keep credentials secure and avoid prompting during execution.
Use specific and relevant search keywords to get the most accurate review results for the business or location you are researching.
Monitor the script output for status updates during long-running tasks and only retry if the script stops producing output for an extended period.
anti_patterns: Hardcoding the API key directly in the script or SKILL.md file instead of using environment variables, which exposes credentials in source control.
Interpreting normal execution progress logs as script failures or deadlocks, since the task can take several minutes and produces status updates every 10 seconds.
Retrying failed tasks repeatedly without first checking if the API key is valid, which wastes time and API resources when the key has expired.

faq.question: What API key is needed to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. You can get a key from the BrowserAct Console at browseract.com.

faq.question: How long does review extraction take?
faq.answer: Extraction can take several minutes because it involves automated browser operations. The script polls for status every 10 seconds and has a 5-minute timeout.

faq.question: What review data is returned?
faq.answer: The skill returns author name, profile URL, star rating, review text, date, like count, and author image URL for each review found.

faq.question: Can I search for reviews in different languages?
faq.answer: Yes. You can set the language parameter to values like en, zh-CN, es, or fr. You can also set the country parameter to bias results to specific regions.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script detects authorization errors and prints an Invalid authorization message. It will not retry the task, and you should provide a valid API key.

faq.question: Can I extract reviews for multiple businesses at once?
faq.answer: Each execution handles one search query. To extract reviews for multiple businesses, run the skill multiple times with different keywords.
